assertions.expectation: Explains this is related to the interaction between maxSurge, drain order, and pod scheduling
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains the interaction between maxSurge being too high, the order of nodes being cordoned/drained, and how the Kubernetes scheduler places pods on available but soon-to-be-drained nodes

assertions.expectation: Notes that GKE cordons nodes before draining them — pods should prefer scheduling onto uncordoned (upgraded) nodes
assertions.reason: Response explains the sequence where GKE cordons multiple old nodes at once and that pods should land on upgraded nodes, with anti-affinity examples targeting uncordoned nodes

assertions.expectation: Identifies possible cause: too many nodes being drained simultaneously (high maxSurge) before enough upgraded nodes exist
assertions.reason: Response explicitly identifies that maxSurge setting is too high relative to cluster size and explains how this causes multiple nodes to be cordoned before upgraded nodes are ready

assertions.expectation: Recommends reducing maxSurge to 1 to ensure upgraded nodes are available before draining the next batch
assertions.reason: Response provides specific gcloud command to set maxSurge to 1 and explains this ensures upgraded nodes are available before the next old node gets cordoned

assertions.expectation: Suggests using pod anti-affinity or node affinity to prefer already-upgraded nodes
assertions.reason: Response provides two specific YAML examples: node affinity to prefer upgraded nodes by kubelet version and anti-affinity to avoid nodes being drained

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if any taints or tolerations are causing pods to land on the wrong nodes
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Clusters with node taints/tolerations: Further restricts where evicted pods can land' as a contributing factor

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl commands to check node cordoned status and pod scheduling events
assertions.reason: Response provides kubectl commands to monitor node status, check which nodes are cordoned, and verify pod placement

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting maxUnavailable=0 to ensure old nodes aren't removed until pods are safely rescheduled
assertions.reason: Response includes --max-unavailable-upgrade 0 in the gcloud command example

assertions.expectation: May mention GKE's autoscaled blue-green upgrade strategy as an alternative that avoids this issue by cordoning the entire old pool before migrating workloads to new nodes
assertions.reason: Response provides detailed explanation of autoscaled blue-green upgrade strategy with specific gcloud commands and explains how it creates the green pool first before draining the blue pool
